business.headline: Can you withdraw Sukanya Samriddhi money before 21 years?Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana is designed for long-term savings, but life doesn't always go as planned. So, can you access your money before the 21-year maturity period? Here's a simple look at the rules.
Emcure shares slip by 2.64%: Did a viral video trigger the fall?While the broader market remained positive, Emcure Pharma's stock moved in the opposite direction.

Sensex, Nifty end flat after wild swings as market waits for clearer signalsThe BSE Sensex closed at 78,520.30, up 26.76 points or 0.03%, while the NSE Nifty50 settled at 24,364.85, higher by 11.30 points or 0.05%.

Should you buy gold now? Here's what this Akshaya Tritiya revealedGold prices are high and markets are uncertain. Yet, this Akshaya Tritiya saw steady buying. So, should you invest now or wait? Here's what buyer trends and market signals are telling us.

Commercial vs residential investing: Which investment gives better returns?For most first-time investors, the confusion is simple. Should you buy a house for steady rent or invest in a shop or office for higher returns? The answer is not straightforward, because both options behave very differently in terms of income, risk and effort.

Why 71% of consumers believe loyalty rewards don't actually foster loyaltyLoyalty has always been about more than transactions – it is about the feeling of being valued. Customers stay not because of points but because a brand makes them feel recognised in ways that matter.

What is Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool? Rs 13,000 crore plan explainedWhy has the government launched the Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool with Rs 13,000 crore? The answer lies in rising war risks, costly shipping routes and protecting trade from global shocks.

DA hiked by 2%: How much extra will you get and how to calculate?The government has raised Dearness Allowance by 2%, but what does it really mean for your monthly salary? Here's a simple look at how much extra you can expect and how the calculation works.

Delhi power bills may rise after tribunal rejects plea on Rs 30,000 crore duesThe dues are linked to pending recoveries payable to Delhi's power distribution companies (discoms) under a larger liquidation plan aimed at clearing long-standing liabilities in the power sector.

ICICI Bank vs HDFC Bank after Q4: Which stock looks like a better buy now?ICICI Bank gained after Q4 results while HDFC Bank slipped, raising a key investor question. Which private banking giant now offers better growth, dividends and value potential? Read on to find out.

Big relief for shipping industry: India clears Rs 13,000 crore insurance poolIndia's shipping sector has long depended on global insurance markets to keep goods moving across oceans. But recent disruptions in key trade routes and rising geopolitical risks have exposed a critical gap.

ICICI Bank rises nearly 2% after Q4 results: Should you buy now?ICICI Bank reported a net profit of about Rs 13,702 crore for the fourth quarter, up 8.5% from a year ago. It also announced a dividend of Rs 12 per share for FY26, higher than the Rs 11 per share declared last year, adding to investor confidence.

HDFC Bank drops 1% after Q4 results: Profit rises, so why is stock down?HDFC Bank reported a net profit of about Rs 19,221 crore for the fourth quarter, up around 9% from a year ago. But shares of India's largest private lender fell in early trade.

Strait of Hormuz crisis returns: Oil prices climb 6% on fresh conflict fearsLatest data shows WTI crude at $88.98, up 6.12%, while Brent crude has climbed to $95.23, up 5.37% as of 9 am. The sudden rise reflects growing concern that oil supply could be hit if tensions continue.

Gold falls Rs 1,700, silver down nearly Rs 5,000: Should you buy now or wait?Gold and silver prices fell sharply on Monday, offering some relief to buyers after the recent rally pushed rates near record highs across retail and futures markets in India.
Sensex, Nifty erase early losses to trade higher amid high volatilityThe S&P BSE Sensex was up 168.96 points to 78,662.50, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 21,05 points to 24,374.60 as of 9:30 am.
Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock markets rise or fall today?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,469.50 at 7:57 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open above Friday's close of 24,353.55.

Why your car insurance may not pay when you need it mostAn accident, a flooded road, a damaged engine, and a car insurance claim is filed with the expectation that the insurer will step in. But for many, that moment turns into something else entirely: confusion, partial payouts, or in some cases, outright rejection.

India's digital payments boom has a growing fraud problemIndia's digital boom has made payments effortless, but also easier to exploit. From fake identities to instant scams, fraudsters are moving faster than the systems meant to stop them, and users are paying the price.

Govt approves 2% dearness allowance hike for central govt employees, pensionersThe Union Cabinet approved a 2% hike in Dearness Allowance for government employees and pensioners, along with key decisions including a Rs 13,000 crore Sovereign Maritime Fund and extension of PMGSY till 2028.

entertainment.headline: Musician D4vd charged with murder of missing 14-year-old girl in Los Angeles Musician D4vd has been charged with the murder of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas. Her remains were found in a car registered to him.

Deven Bhojani says Sarabhai vs Sarabhai cannot happen without Satish Shah Actor-director Deven Bhojani reflected on how Sarabhai vs Sarabhai went from low TRPs to becoming a cult favourite over the years. He also explained why a full revival is unlikely now, despite past attempts to bring the beloved sitcom back.

Dhurandhar 2's blockbuster month in cinemas: What it means for the industry Within just a month at the box office, Dhurandhar: The Revenge has not just broken records. It has redefined the grammar of what a film's "good run" looks like.

Aishwarya Rai celebrates 19 years with Abhishek Bachchan, shares family pic Actor Aishwarya Rai celebrated 19 years with her actor-husband Abhishek Bachchan by sharing a family photo on Instagram. The post also featured their daughter Aaradhya.

Genelia tears up as Riteish Deshmukh recalls struggle behind Raja Shivaji film Genelia D'Souza turned emotional at the Raja Shivaji trailer launch as Riteish Deshmukh spoke about the film's journey and struggles. The historical drama on Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj is set to release on May 1.

Lawrence of Punjab trailer: New documentary series on power, music and criminal life The trailer of the upcoming documentary series Lawrence of Punjab explores the cultural context of crime in Punjab. It uses gangster Lawrence Bishnoi's journey as a case study of politics, music, ideology and media amplification.

Asha Bhosle's ashes immersed in Varanasi, granddaughter Zanai breaks down. Video Asha Bhosle's ashes were immersed in Varanasi as per her last wishes. Her granddaughter Zanai got emotional during the rituals.

Shekhar Kapur's My Name Is Memory series to span wars, conflict and empires Filmmaker Shekhar Kapur is set to adapt Ann Brashares's novel, My Name Is Memory, into a series of the same name that he will direct and produce. Kapur described the book as a beautiful and intense love story that transcends and survives internal and external conflicts.

PM Modi notes rise of Korean wave in India, announces friendship festival in 2028 Prime Minister Narendra Modi acknowledged the growing popularity of K-pop and K-dramas in India. He announced an India-South Korea Friendship Festival for 2028.

Raja Shivaji trailer: Riteish Deshmukh fights for Maratha pride in period drama Riteish Deshmukh stars in the title role of the upcoming period drama, Raja Shivaji. The trailer of the Marathi film, co-written and directed by the actor, was released on Monday.

Vijay Deverakonda, Shouryuv's film begins with pooja ceremony, Nani attends VDxShouryuv, starring Vijay Deverakonda, began with a traditional pooja ceremony on April 20. Actor Nani also attended the ceremony. The team's scale has already created early buzz around the film.

Beef Season 2 review: Plays it safe, loses the bite that made Season 1 addictiveRelease Date:Thu Apr 16, 2026 Beef Season 2 series review: Beef Season 2 presents a quieter, more introspective follow-up to the intense and chaotic first season, focusing on simmering resentments and complex relationships rather than explosive drama.

Drishyam 3 director reacts to TV serial-like execution jibe, defends his choices Mollywood director Jeethu Joseph responded to criticism over his films' technical quality and lighting style. He revealed that Drishyam 3 will have a fresh narrative and release on May 21.

Ramayana sparks global blockbuster talk at CinemaCon: Exhibitor weighs in Global exhibitor John Fithian expressed that Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana could become India's first global blockbuster. Early footage screened at CinemaCon 2026 has sparked strong worldwide buzz.

Vijay blesses newlyweds with gift during political rally. Watch the Video Vijay blessed newlyweds with a gift during an election rally. The actor was campaigning for his party, Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), ahead of the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections.

Yash shares Toxic story details, says it captures darkest emotions, dilemmas Yash shared new insights into Toxic: A Fairy Tale for Grown-Ups, revealing its layered storytelling and emotionally intense core. The film, he said, promises more than just action, exploring deeper human dilemmas in a unique way.

Ajay Devgn, Kajol shower love on daughter Nysa on her birthday, share rare pics Ajay Devgn and Kajol celebrated daughter Nysa Devgan's 23rd birthday with heartfelt throwback posts and loving messages. The couple shared childhood memories and wishes, making the occasion special for their daughter.

Days of Our Lives actor Patrick Muldoon dies at 57 after suffering heart attack Patrick Muldoon, best known for Days of Our Lives, died at the age of 57 after suffering a heart attack. The actor had a long career in television and films, and was also active as a producer and musician.

Anushka-Virat seek blessings from Premanand Maharaj on Akshaya Tritiya. Video Anushka Sharma and Virat Kohli were spotted at a Vrindavan ashram, and videos from their visit are now going viral. The couple's spiritual side and recent public moments have once again caught attention.

general news.headline: I'm winning war by a lot: Trump declares as Iran talks teeter on edgeAt a time when a second round of talks is expected in Pakistan, uncertainty continues to cloud both participation and intent. While Trump insists progress is being made militarily and diplomatically, Iran has raised serious concerns about US actions.

Justice doesn't bow to pressure: Judge refuses to withdraw from Kejriwal's caseDelhi High Court Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma refused to withdraw from the Delhi liquor policy case, rejecting allegations of bias raised by AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal, asserting that judges do not bow to political pressure.

Lots of bombs start going off: Trump warns Iran if ceasefire ends without a dealDonald Trump warned Iran as the US delegation prepared to return to the negotiating table for the second round of talks in Pakistan amid uncertainty over Tehran's participation.

The miracle at India's petrol pumpsWith oil on fire due to the war in the Middle East, all big economies have raised petrol and diesel prices. However, pump prices of normal petrol and diesel in India have stayed unchanged since May 2022. This is what's behind this miracle.

Make-or-break talks: Iran backs out, US pushes ahead, and a hopeful Pak preparesAs a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran nears its April 22 deadline, the hopes of extending it and cooling-off tensions hinge on a second round of talks in Islamabad. Tehran says it won't be attending, while Washington is pushing ahead. Pakistan, meanwhile, is preparing for talks that might not happen at all.

Swami Vivekananda said give me blood...: Yogi Adityanath goofs up, TMC trolls himUttar Pradesh CM Yogi Adityanath made a goof-up by misattributing the famous "Tum mujhe khoon do, main tumhe azaadi dunga" slogan – originally coined by Subhas Chandra Bose – to Swami Vivekananda. The TMC mocked him, with MP Mahua Moitra, dubbing him as "bulldozer buddhi".

I-PAC announces big changes in Bengal ops after TMC rejects firm's shutdown buzzThe Indian Political Action Committee (I-PAC) has introduced major operational changes in West Bengal, including shifting some teams to work-from-home and restricting official communication channels. This comes a day after the Trinamool Congress dismissed reports of the firm shutting down in the state.

Live: Trump says he's 'under no pressure' to strike Iran deal, blasts criticsUS-Iran-Israel War Live Updates: Iran refused to hold the second round of the ceasefire talks with the United States, citing Washington's "excessive demands, unrealistic expectations, constant shifts in stance, repeated contradictions, and the naval blockade of the maritime chokepoint, Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, Donald Trump said that the US delegation team, led by Vice President JD Vance, will arrive in Islamabad today. However, after Iran's refusal, further ceasefire talks regarding the prevailing West Asia conflict look bleak. Amid the developments, Pakistan's Prime Minister, Shehbaz Sharif, held a phone call with Iran's President, Masoud Pezeshkian, and discussed the regional situation. He also briefed Pezeshkian on the conversation the South Asian country had with Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Turkey. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

How Gulf countries paid $142 billion to US for a leaky THAAD umbrellaGulf countries poured billions into American missile defences designed to stop Iran's arsenal. Yet when retaliation came, sheer volume and cost asymmetry turned the equation. The Middle East countries were left with charred THAAD systems and a costly lesson.

Local stall to viral fame: PM's jhalmuri stop turns vendor into overnight sensationVikram Kumar's jhalmuri stall in Jhargram has drawn massive crowds a day after Prime Minister Narendra Modi's unscheduled stop during his West Bengal election campaign visit.

Command and Central Calculus: The Lady's Challenge | Part 46There was something almost old-fashioned about the way Jayalalithaa governed after 2011. Not democratic in the chatty, consultative, hand-holding sense that modern political brochures like to advertise, but old-fashioned in the sterner, more Indian sense — rule as authority, government as command, bureaucracy as instrument.

Watch: After Vijay's hologram, TVK's robot steals the show in CoimbatoreDays after Vijay's hologram debuted at a rally, a TVK candidate in Coimbatore deployed a robot for campaigning. Sethil Kumar, TVK's candidate from Coimbatore South, turned heads on Racecourse Road on Monday morning by deploying a robot dressed in TVK colours for canvassing.

Delhi's Aditya Gupta bags AIR 1 in JEE Main, credits 12-hour study plan, aims IIT DelhiAditya Gupta has secured AIR 1 in JEE Main 2026 Session 2, emerging as the national topper. His success story highlights effective preparation strategies, consistency, and strong mental discipline, key factors for cracking one of India's toughest exams.

For political mileage: Court blasts BJP worker in Rahul Gandhi citizenship caseBJP worker Vignesh Shishir has approached the Allahabad High Court seeking registration of an FIR against Rahul Gandhi, claiming he possesses documents purportedly proving the Congress leader's British citizenship and seeking his disqualification from Parliament.

Telugu duo in US face 5-year jail for H-1B fraud; hired people for ghost jobsTwo Indian-origin Telugu men in California admitted to committing H1-B visa fraud. The duo hired people for jobs at the University of California that never existed. They now face 5 years in jail in the US.

Video: Man faints and falls into swimming pool, drowns as bystanders fail to helpA family celebration turned tragic at a Rajkot resort when a 33-year-old man drowned after he fainted and fell into a swimming pool. The victim's death was initially believed to have been caused by drowning, though police are probing how he fainted.

The legend of the white towel on sarkari kursiThe white towel on government office chairs may look like an everyday thing, but it carries a long history of colonial habits, heat, hygiene and hierarchy. Here is how a simple cloth became one of India's most recognisable symbols of official power.

Catch-22 for me, win-win for Kejriwal: What Justice Sharma said on recusal pleaWhile pronouncing the order, Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma made a series of pointed observations on the recusal plea and its implications for the judiciary.

Kirpans out, turbans in air, gun fired as Germany gurdwara sees violent clashA violent clash involving over 40 people at a gurdwara in Germany's Moers left at least 11 injured and triggered a major police operation, with elite commandos deployed amid reports of gunfire. Kirpans were flashed and turbans were in the air amid violence stemming from a long-running internal dispute.

Serious matter against you, says suspension letter of TCS Nashik accused Nida KhanA suspension letter issued to Nida Khan, an absconding accused in the Nashik TCS-linked sexual harassment and alleged conversion case, has surfaced, confirming she was suspended on April 9 after the company cited a "serious matter" and her inability to work.

Aishwarya Rai celebrates 19 years with Abhishek Bachchan, shares family picActor Aishwarya Rai celebrated 19 years with her actor-husband Abhishek Bachchan by sharing a family photo on Instagram. The post also featured their daughter Aaradhya.

Pak cleric Engineer Mirza dodges death 12th time, delivers lecture after being shot atPakistan's controversial cleric-YouTuber Engineer Muhammad Ali Mirza survived an attack by a gunman, in what is being counted as the 12th attempt on his life. Even after the attack, the cleric carried on with his lecture in Punjab's Jhelum. Mirza has been targeted over his remarks on sectarian orthodoxy in Pakistan.

This 58-year-old dadi almost quit mid-race. Then came a Hyrox podium finishA grandmother in her late 50s overcame self-doubt and tough competition to secure a podium finish at the Bengaluru Hyrox event.

Ghaziabad woman shares Rapido driver's inappropriate messages, company reactsA woman from Ghaziabad has shared screenshots of a WhatsApp conversation allegedly initiated by the Rapido bike driver who had dropped her.

sports.headline: India Women's World Cup champions miss out on Laureus glory as PSG win top honour India's Women's World Cup-winning team missed out on the Laureus Team of the Year award, with Paris Saint-Germain taking top honours after a dominant 2025 season.

Proud of myself: Tilak rejoices after maiden IPL hundred ends MI's wait for victory IPL 2026, GT vs MI: Tilak Varma smashed a brilliant unbeaten 101 off 45 balls to power Mumbai Indians to a 99-run win over Gujarat Titans and match MI's fastest IPL hundred record.

Not Hardik's problem: MI captain hits back at critics over Bumrah first-over call IPL 2026, GT vs MI: Back to winning ways after four defeats in a row, a relieved Mumbai Indians skipper Hardik Pandya took a dig at critics who had questioned his decision not to use Jasprit Bumrah with the new ball.

Jasprit Bumrah back to his best: Could this be the start of a comeback season? Jasprit Bumrah rediscovered his rhythm as he led Mumbai Indians to a dominant 99-run win over Gujarat Titans in IPL 2026. After a lean start to the season, he struck early with the wicket of Sai Sudharsan and helped MI snap their four-match losing streak.

Tilak Varma's special reignites Mumbai Indians campaign, routs GT IPL 2026, GT vs MI: Tilak Varma turned an evening that seemed to be slipping away into one to remember, as Mumbai Indians snapped their losing streak with a crushing 99-run win over Gujarat Titans in Ahmedabad.

Brazilian triathlete Mara Flavia, 38, dies during Ironman Texas swim leg Brazilian triathlete and influencer Mara Flávia Araujo died during the opening swim leg of Ironman Texas in The Woodlands on Saturday, in an incident that has sent shockwaves through the endurance sports community.

Tilak Varma smashes 45-ball century, flaunts new celebration in Ahmedabad IPL 2026: Tilak Varma led a stunning Mumbai Indians revival with a blistering 45-ball century against Gujarat Titans at the Narendra Modi Stadium, smashing his way to a record-equalling fastest MI hundred after a slow start and powering the team to a 200-run total.

After Blessing Muzarabani, PCB punishes Dasun Shanaka for PSL to IPL switch The Pakistan Cricket Board banned Dasun Shanaka for one year from the Pakistan Super League after he withdrew to join the Indian Premier League, breaching his contract with Lahore Qalandars.

MS Dhoni fandom 'sycophancy': Manjrekar takes aim at CSK's handling of legend Sanjay Manjrekar hit out at MS Dhoni's fandom and blamed the Chennai Super Kings management for their struggles in IPL 2026, while also questioning whether the veteran's return from injury would offer any real solution.

Danish Malewar and Krish Bhagat make their IPL debut: Who are MI's new recruits? IPL 2026: Mumbai Indians handed debuts to young players Danish Malewar and Krish Bhagat in their Indian Premier League 2026 match against Gujarat Titans at the Narendra Modi Stadium. Malewar is a promising Vidarbha batter with strong domestic form, while Bhagat is a Punjab all-rounder who impressed as a support bowler in MI's setup before earning his maiden call-up.

IPL 2026: How PBKS opener Priyansh Arya trained to overcome second-season blues Priyansh Arya has leaned on a strong support system to handle the demands of his second Indian Premier League season. While Ricky Ponting helps fine-tune his batting at Punjab Kings, his childhood coach Sanjay Bhardwaj has focused on strengthening his mindset to overcome the challenges of 'second-season blues'.

Ex-Afghanistan pacer Shapoor Zadran hospitalised in Delhi with critical illnessShapoor Zadran is critically ill in a New Delhi hospital after being diagnosed with the rare condition Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis, with complications including tuberculosis and a brain infection. After briefly recovering, the former Afghanistan pacer suffered a relapse and is now in intensive care undergoing treatment.

Faf du Plessis explains impact of India's T20 World Cup win on MI's slumpIPL 2026: Faf du Plessis hinted that Mumbai Indians' poor start to the Indian Premier League 2026 season may be linked to a dip in hunger among Indian players following their T20 World Cup triumph. He noted that several World Cup-winning players, including Hardik Pandya and Jasprit Bumrah, have struggled for form, reflecting a wider trend across the tournament.

GT vs MI, Highlights: Mumbai snap losing streak, hammer Gujarat by 99 runsGujarat (GT) vs Mumbai (MI) Highlights, IPL 2026 today match: Mumbai Indians beat the Gujarat Titans by 99 runs at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad to snap their four-match losing streak. Tilak Varma scored a 45-ball century after which Ashwani Kumar took four wickets.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is one for the future: Shreyas praises 'spectacular' RR starPunjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer has heaped praise on Rajasthan Royals' young star Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, describing him as a "spectacular talent" and predicting a bright future after his impressive run in IPL 2026.

Red flag for Indian athletics: India placed in highest-risk doping bracketIndia has been moved to the highest-risk 'Category A' bracket for doping after consistently recording the most violations globally, forcing the World Athletics body's Athletics Integrity Unit to impose stringent new testing protocols.

IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma switches to Hitman mode in MI nets ahead of GT clashIPL 2026: Rohit Sharma returned to nets ahead of MI's clash with Gujarat Titans, raising hopes of a comeback. With Mumbai Indians struggling, his return could be the spark needed to revive their IPL 2026 campaign.

Hardik, when was your last match-winning knock? MI skipper urged to step upIPL 2026: Hardik Pandya faces mounting pressure as Mumbai Indians struggle to find winning momentum this season. Aakash Chopra questioned the skipper's lack of a recent match-winning knock, urging him to step up.

Aren't you ashamed? Ex-KKR star slams classist trolling of Rinku SinghIPL 2026: Former India opener Aakash Chopra said he was delighted for Rinku Singh after his match-winning knock for Kolkata Knight Riders against Rajasthan Royals, adding that the batter had faced classist trolling on social media.

Decide on posthumous Padma Vibhushan for KD Jadhav by May 4: High Court to CentreWrestler Khashaba Jadhav, who passed away in 1984, had won the bronze medal in the Helsinki Olympic Games held in 1952. He was awarded the Arjuna Award posthumously in 2001.

Riyan Parag will make his comeback: RR coach backs skipper despite lean patchIPL 2026: Riyan Parag continues to endure a lean IPL 2026 run, but Rajasthan Royals remain firmly behind their skipper. Batting coach Vikram Rathour insists it is only a matter of time before he turns it around.

Who is Yash Raj Punja? Ex-UAE cricketer's brother impresses on IPL debutIPL 2026, KKR vs RR: Yash Raj Punja impressed on his IPL debut for Rajasthan Royals, returning with figures of 4-0-25-1 against Kolkata Knight Riders on Sunday at the Eden Gardens in Kolkata.

Alone by the boundary ropes, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in tears after RR's loss to KKRIPL 2026: Rajasthan Royals' teenage superstar Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was spotted in tears near the boundary rope after their loss to Kolkata Knight Riders at Eden Gardens. The youngster's 28-ball 46 went in vain as KKR restricted Rajasthan to 155 before successfully chasing the target.

Mikel Arteta sticks to 'I believe' as Arsenal's loss to City tightens title raceGunners manager Mikel Arteta stuck to his "I believe" message despite Arsenal's loss to Manchester City tightening the title race. With just five games left, the pressure is now firmly on both sides.

Forged in Chennai, unleashed in Hyderabad: Inside Praful Hinge and Sakib's riseIPL 2026: The MRF Pace Foundation in Chennai continues to shape India's fast-bowling future, with its rigorous system producing talents like Praful Hinge and Sakib Hussain, now making their mark for SunRisers Hyderabad.

Pressure mounts on Hardik Pandya as MI's ship faces stormy Ahmedabad test vs GTIPL 2026, GT vs MI: Mumbai Indians have lost four games on the trot and will be eager to return to winning ways against Gujarat Titans at a venue where they are yet to register a victory over them.

Saw KKR dressing room in tears, many made baseless judgements about us: ChakravarthyIPL 2026, KKR vs RR: Varun Chakravarthy revealed emotional scenes in KKR's dressing room after their first IPL 2026 win, crediting team resilience amid criticism. Varun's match-winning spell and Rinku Singh's heroics sealed a dramatic chase against Rajasthan Royals in Kolkata on Sunday.

Roman Reigns beats CM Punk as WrestleMania 42 Night 2 delivers chaosRoman Reigns defeated CM Punk in a clean main event to win the World Heavyweight title. WrestleMania 42 Night 2 also saw multiple title changes and a potential farewell moment for Brock Lesnar.

Lalit Modi claims IPL team owner did black magic after bizarre SRH vs CSK episodeIPL 2026: A viral 'black magic' controversy involving Chennai Super Kings and Sunrisers Hyderabad was dismissed as fake by Lalit Modi. However, his remarks hinted that such bizarre claims may not be entirely new in IPL history.

IPL Play Of The Day: Pant goes full Ghajini as PBKS plunder LSG in MullanpurIPL 2026: It was a night to forget for Rishabh Pant, the LSG skipper, as his decision to not use main spinner M. Siddharth effectively came to haunt them the match against PBKS. Pant also had a howler with DRS, which gave Cooper Connolly a second life.

Happy ending: Ricky Ponting hugs Shashank Singh, moves past anger over fielding lapsesIPL 2026, PBKS vs LSG Ricky Ponting shared a warm hug with Shashank Singh after the match, putting aside his earlier frustration over the batter's costly fielding lapses. On Sunday, Punjab Kings beat the Lucknow Super Giants by 54 runs.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
